steps.title: Find p from the given point
steps.content:
Since the graph passes through $(-3, 0)$, we have $h(-3) = 0$. Substituting $x = -3$:
$$(-3+p)(-3-2)(2(-3)-12) = 0$$
Simplifying the constant factors:
$$(-3+p)(-5)(-18) = 0$$
Since $-5 \neq 0$ and $-18 \neq 0$, the only way the product can be zero is if $-3 + p = 0$, giving us $p = 3$.

steps.title: Evaluate h(0) with p = 3
steps.content:
With $p = 3$, the function becomes $h(x) = (x+3)(x-2)(2x-12)$. Substituting $x = 0$:
$$h(0) = (0+3)(0-2)(2 \cdot 0 - 12)$$
$$h(0) = (3)(-2)(-12)$$
Computing the product: $(3)(-2) = -6$, and then $(-6)(-12) = 72$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
